evidence_basis: Kate Elisabeth YouTube channel RSS feed snapshot for current uploads.
Current YouTube search-result page for London weekly-spending queries on 2026-04-23.
Current watch-page metadata for the selected video and the nearest older competitor.
Manual review of the selected video frames and audio for visible price overlays and spoken totals.

selection_snapshot.query_used: kate elisabeth how much i spend in a week london

latest_matching_video.title: How much I Spend £ in a Week as a 25 year-old Living in London 🇬🇧
latest_matching_video.selection_rationale: On the 2026-04-23 snapshot, this is the newest Kate Elisabeth long-form upload whose title explicitly states a London weekly-spending framing.

older_relevant_candidates.title: What I Spend £ in a Week as a 25 year old Living and Working in London 🇬🇧 (bills, transport, food)
older_relevant_candidates.status: Relevant but older than the hidden baseline selection.

video_framing.theme: weekly personal spending while living in London
video_framing.description_hint: if you’ve read this far comment: 'she didn't need to spend that much'
video_framing.page_summary_snapshot: A 25-year-old documents a week of spending in London, from chai lattes to fitness/gym spending, shopping trips, transport, meals, and nights out.

spend_row_policy.remainder_policy: The video states a grand total of £542.94. The visible/explicit itemized rows below sum to £520.68, leaving £22.26 unitemized or not confidently visible in the sampled evidence. A candidate may report this as unitemized_or_unconfirmed_remainder, but should not invent a named spend category for it without video evidence.

canonical_spend_rows.evidence_notes: Visible overlay is £9.95; creator later rounds it verbally to £10.
Food shop amount appears as a £91 overlay and is discussed again later.
Payment machine shows Please pay: £85.00 and pay-as-you-go credit top-up.
Visible £2.90 overlay at the Tube gate.

canonical_spend_rows.item: Natural Fitness Food wrap and drink
canonical_spend_rows.evidence_notes: Visible £11.95 overlay on the gym food and drink shot.
Visible £35 overlay on the dumpling dinner.
Visible and spoken £4.95 for the bakery item.
Visible £2.90 overlay during the return journey.
Visible £2.16 overlay on the bike ride shot.
Creator discusses shopping and visible overlay shows £150.
Visible book overlay is £14.95; creator rounds to basically £15.
Visible £2.90 overlay at a Tube gate later in the vlog.
Visible £21.73 overlay in a cab scene.

canonical_spend_rows.item: Pre-drinks shop / mixers and energy drinks
canonical_spend_rows.evidence_notes: Self-checkout subtotal shows £13.15 for two items.
Visible £16.50 overlay on the pasta dinner.

totals.grand_total_note: The creator states the grand total as £542.94 for the documented days and says this does not include mortgage, water, or electricity.

non_spend_mentions.policy: Do not count as a spend row unless a candidate finds a video-supported price; it appears as hosted/free context rather than an explicit personal spend.
Do not count as a spend row unless a candidate finds a video-supported price; free/hosted events should be described as non-spend context.

expected_output_contract.currency_expectation: Rows should use GBP / £ unless the creator explicitly quotes another currency and converts it.

subtitle_policy.allowed_auxiliary_use: Browser captions while watching may help disambiguate spoken words, but downloaded subtitles/transcripts are not valid evidence and cannot compensate for missing video-frame or audio-timestamp evidence.
subtitle_policy.why_strict: Many canonical spend rows are visible price overlays only and do not appear in captions. Subtitle-only extraction misses Oyster top-up, several TfL fares, gym food, Third Space allocation, Zara, taxi rides, mixers, and dinner overlays.

common_mistakes: Selecting the older July 2025 London spending video instead of the newer September 2025 one.
Using downloaded subtitles or a transcript as the primary data source.
Producing the old 5-6 row answer containing only breakfast, groceries, bun, clothes, books, and maybe dumplings.
Missing visible price-overlay rows for transport, gym/fitness, taxi, Zara, mixers, or pasta dinner.
Treating the stated £542.94 grand total as fully itemized without explaining the £22.26 remainder.
Mixing inferred budget categories with explicit spend mentions without labeling them.
supervisor_note: This task should grade video-grounded extraction. Subtitles alone are insufficient because the critical benchmark behavior is finding and reading visual price overlays across the vlog.
